Title: Forgoing Raya with family, district police chief in Penang visit stations under him
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: As a district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad could well have spent the entire first day of Aidilfitri with his family.
Instead, he chose to spend a good part of the day visiting six police stations under his district.
The Northeast police chief probably spent the morning with his family, but from noon till 3pm, he went to to the Jalan Patani, Central, Tanjung Tokong, Lebuh Pantai, Datuk Keramat and Jelutong police stations.
Rozak said the visit was aimed at observing the duties of frontline personnel on the first day of Aidilfitri.
"It also sought to engage with and motivate personnel who are unable to take leave and remain on duty during the festive period," he said.
During the visit, Rozak also distributed festive cookies to personnel on duty as a gesture of appreciation for their commitment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt urged to set up National Harmony Commission to guard unity
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has been urged to set up a National Harmony Commission to serve as an independent statutory watchdog for the nation's inter-ethnic and interfaith relations.
Prominent national unity advocate Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said the commission's primary role should include advising the government on sensitive policies and addressing hate speech and incitement.
Lee noted that Malaysia's pluralism is foundational to the nation's birth and warned that current tensions require a proactive, institutionalised response.
"I strongly call for the establishment of a National Harmony Commission as an independent statutory body dedicated to safeguarding and strengthening inter-ethnic and interfaith harmony in our country.
"In recent years, we have witnessed increasing instances of racial and religious sensitivities being tested both online and offline, which, if left unchecked, may erode the very foundation of our national unity," he said in a statement today.
Lee, who is the chairman of the Alliance for a Safe Community, stressed that such a commission is crucial to diffuse racial tension before isolated incidents escalate into larger conflicts.
"A National Harmony Commission would serve as an early intervention mechanism in monitoring trends, mediating disputes, and addressing grievances before they spiral into serious unrest.
"Harmony cannot be sustained through laws alone. It requires continuous dialogue, education, and engagement. The commission could facilitate structured interfaith and intercultural dialogues, promote community-based initiatives, and recommend policies that foster inclusivity and mutual respect," he added.
He said the body could provide objective, evidence-based research on policies touching on race, religion, language, and education.
While enforcement agencies maintain law and order, Lee highlighted the need for restorative and educational approaches, where the commission could recommend non-punitive interventions and develop guidelines to curb incitement without undermining legitimate freedom of expression.
Addressing the rapid spread of inflammatory content online, he suggested the commission work closely with civil society, media platforms, and educational institutions to promote digital responsibility.
"Since independence under the leadership of Tunku Abdul Rahman, our nation has been built upon the principles of moderation, compromise, and respect for diversity. These principles must be actively protected and nurtured.
"The establishment of such a commission should not be seen as a reaction to the crisis, but as a proactive step towards nation-building.
"Its composition must reflect Malaysia's diversity and include respected individuals of integrity from various communities, as well as experts in law, sociology, conflict resolution, and human rights.
"As chairman of the Alliance for a Safe Community, I firmly believe that safety is not merely the absence of crime — it is the presence of trust, mutual respect, and social cohesion," he said.
Previously, numerous quarters have called for the establishment of the commission to address racial issues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Running app gives away location of French aircraft carrier in Mediterranean
Article:
Paris, France: A member of the French navy using an app to track his jogging performance broadcast the exact position of his country's flagship aircraft carrier, a newspaper has reported.
France deployed the Charles de Gaulle – and accompanying frigates – to the Mediterranean in early March shortly after US-Israeli strikes on Iran sparked war in the Middle East.
It has been in the eastern Mediterranean since March 9 as part of what Emmanuel Macron has called a "purely defensive" posture in support of France's allies in the conflict.
Le Monde newspaper reported on Thursday that the runner jogged in circles on a ship in movement on March 13 in the middle of the sea northwest of Cyprus, according to his public profile on the Strava fitness tracking app, while satellite images showed the aircraft carrier was in the immediate vicinity at the time.
The same person had also been running in Copenhagen, Denmark, in late February, across a bridge from Malmo, Sweden, where the Charles de Gaulle was anchored at the time, Strava data showed.
The French armed forces told AFP appropriate measures would be taken if the report was true, as members of the navy were regularly reminded about the risk of security breaches using such apps.
"The reported case – if confirmed – does not comply with the current instructions," it said.
Running app profiles have given away sensitive information before.
In 2024, Le Monde reported that the bodyguards of Macron, the US president Joe Biden and Russian leader Vladimir Putin were inadvertently giving away information about their whereabouts while accompanying them on trips.
In 2018, Strava maps showed the locations of US and allied military personnel in Iraq, Syria and Afghanistan.
While some bases were well known to groups that might want to attack them, the maps also showed what appear to be routes taken by forces moving outside of bases – information that could have been used in planning bombings or ambushes.
mra/ah/yad
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Factory operator shot in chest after roadside argument
Article:
KULIM: A 24-year-old factory operator was injured after being shot in the arm and chest during a heated argument in Padang Serai last night.
The incident occurred at 11.50pm in front of a house in Kampung Permatang Durian, Seberang Paya.
Kulim deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the victim was reportedly on his way home when he was intercepted by a 42-year-old suspect.
"The suspect had earlier contacted the victim, asking him to meet at his house in Kampung Naga Lilit. However, the suspect intercepted the victim at the scene, where a verbal altercation broke out.
"The suspect then fired a single shot from a revolver, hitting the victim in the right arm and chest," he said when contacted today.
The victim was rushed to Kulim Hospital for treatment. Following the incident, police tracked down and arrested the suspect, a lorry driver, in front of his home in Kampung Naga Lilit.
Officers seized a revolver, 10 rounds of live ammunition, two shell casings, and a Honda Civic believed to have been used in the crime.
Investigations revealed that both men have extensive criminal records.
The suspect has 11 prior criminal offences and three drug-related offences, while the victim has six prior narcotic offences and other criminal entries.
The case is being investigated under Section 3 of the Firearms (Increased Penalties) Act 1971 and Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Raya in rehab: Three youths share regret, hope for a second chance
Article:
KOTA BARU: While Muslims nationwide celebrated Hari Raya with loved ones, three young men marked the occasion behind the walls of a rehabilitation centre.
Faris, Iskandar and Ahmad (not their real names) are among 123 clients at the Kota Baru Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centre (Puspen) spending the festive season away from home.
For 22-year-old Faris, a former eatery worker from Felda Jengka, this is his first Raya in isolation.
He voluntarily admitted himself six months ago, driven by a growing fear for his life.
"I'm sorry... it was my own mistake. I just wanted to try it for fun," he said, his voice trembling with regret.
Faris revealed he became addicted to "magic mushrooms" inhaled via a vape device, purchasing the substance from a friend for RM20 per ml.
What began as experimentation soon turned into dependence.
"I would use it for 30 minutes to an hour, about 1ml a day. If I didn't take it, I felt restless and stressed," he added.
"I was scared I might die. That's why I came here on my own," he said, adding that he now deeply regrets his actions and the thought of missing Raya with his family this year.
Iskandar, 26, described his journey with ketum as one filled with poor choices and influence from friends.
"I've made many mistakes. I want to change and become a better person," he said.
Iskandar said he began consuming ketum in June last year.
"At one point, I was taking up to two bottles a day. It cost about RM30. I got involved because of friends," he said.
Coming from a middle-income family, Iskandar said he voluntarily entered rehabilitation in hopes of turning his life around.
"I've made many mistakes. I want to change and become a better person. I regret everything and just want a second chance," he said.
The youngest among them, Ahmad, 16, from Pasir Puteh, shared how he fell into drug use at a very early age.
He started taking methamphetamine pills, commonly known as "pil kuda", which he obtained from friends near his home when he was just in Standard Five.
The youngest of seven siblings, he was arrested at his house in July last year.
"I've made many mistakes. I hope my mother can accept me again," he said softly.
As they undergo treatment, the three now spend their festive days in reflection, hoping that this Raya marks the beginning of a permanent departure from their past mistakes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian motorcyclist caught with 14kg heroin in Singapore bust
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian motorcyclist was arrested after attempting to smuggle more than 14 kilogrammes of heroin and other drugs into Singapore, one of two cases foiled at the republic's land checkpoints this week.
Singapore's Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) said the 29-year-old man was stopped at the Tuas Checkpoint on March 17 after officers directed his Malaysian-registered motorcycle for an enhanced inspection.
In a Facebook post, the ICA said checks on the vehicle uncovered multiple black bundles hidden in the rear storage box.
The authorities alerted the Central Narcotics Bureau (CNB), which discovered a total of 25 bundles believed to contain controlled drugs.
The seizure included approximately 14kg of heroin, 2.6kg of methamphetamine, commonly known as 'Ice', and 190g of Ecstasy.
The suspect was arrested and handed over to the CNB for further investigation.
In a separate case on March 18, ICA officers at Woodlands Checkpoint flagged a passenger in a Malaysian-registered vehicle for additional checks.
A bundle suspected to contain drugs was found concealed along the man's waistline near his groin.
Further checks by CNB uncovered drug paraphernalia, while about 81g of cannabis was seized.
A 29-year-old Singaporean man was arrested in connection with the case.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pertiwi soup kitchen brings Raya cheer to KL's homeless
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pertiwi Soup Kitchen spread festive cheer on the first day of Aidilfitri today by distributing special meals to the homeless and urban poor in the city centre.
Volunteers began preparing 240 pre-packaged meals at 11am at the Al Mubarak restaurant in Medan Tuanku.
The festive spread featured nasi briyani, chicken rendang, nasi impit, fish and kuih-muih, served with sirap bandung.
Distribution started at 12.30pm at the Pusat Khidmat Gelandangan Medan Tuanku, where about 100 people queued to collect the special rations.
Pertiwi president Laili Ismail said the organisation has been serving Hari Raya meals for 15 years to bring joy to those often overlooked.
"We have been serving Hari Raya meals for the last 15 years. It's some joy that we can bring to the homeless during the festivities," she told the New Straits Times.
She said most recipients come from around the city centre.
"We usually get around 100 people for the meals. For Hari Raya, we give out two packets instead of one during normal operations.
"We are also grateful to our sponsors and partners who always support this effort."
Within an hour, all the food packets were snapped up by the homeless.
Among the recipients was 74-year-old Sue Ng, formerly from Kampung Kerinchi, Bangsar.
"I usually come to the centre at least three times a week, but if I can make it, I have a bad knee.
"I'm very happy to get this special Hari Raya meal. Everyone here is very nice."
Ng said she has been homeless for the last five years, after her settlement was demolished.
"I was squatting in Kampung Kerinchi for many years. Then, when the area was slated for development, my home was demolished.
"I asked Kuala Lumpur City Hall for low-cost flats, but they don't allow individuals to stay. They wanted to put me in an old folks' home, but I said no.
"So now, I live around the KL Sentral area, seeking shelter wherever I can find it."
Another recipient, 44-year-old Sofia Kurnia, said she comes daily to the centre to collect food with her 11-year-old son.
"I'm not working and separated from my husband. I'm currently living at a friend's place in Titiwangsa.
"We are very grateful to the organisation for providing these meals."
Laili also thanked all the volunteers who tirelessly helped with preparations and distribution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Families turn iconic Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad into colourful Raya photo spot
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Vibrant Hari Raya outfits added a burst of colour to Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad as crowds gathered for photos in front of the historic building.
The scorching sun and humidity did little to deter families, including those with infants and toddlers, from celebrating at the iconic landmark.
Many were seen setting up mobile phones on tripods, while others recorded traditional dance videos to the accompaniment of festive songs.
Muhammad Syafiiq Azman Shah, 32, from Klang, and his extended family of 20 began their day with prayers at Masjid Negara before headed to Dataran Merdeka for a picnic featuring traditional Bugis dishes such as burasak and ayam lengkuas.
Syafiiq, who recently married, said the outing was an opportunity to introduce the city to his relatives from Sabah and Terengganu.
"This year is extra special because we can tour the inside of Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad. Since we are in Kuala Lumpur, we want to make the most of it. Next, we are heading to Menara KL and Putrajaya. We will visit our families for Raya tomorrow," he said.
Retiree Ramli Awang brought his three teenage daughters for their annual family photographs, though his wife, a government clinic nurse, was on duty.
"We could not make it back to Kota Bharu, Kelantan, this year. We had a small celebration at home, exchanging salam Raya before my wife left for work at 7am.
"We enjoyed nasi hujan panas, ayam masak merah and our favourite, ketupat sotong, before setting out," said Ramli.
Nigerian students Ahmad Abdulmutallib, 24, and Muhajid Lawan Gadanya, 26, performed their prayers at Masjid Jamek before making their way to Dataran Merdeka.
The business administration students from Universiti Islam Antarabangsa Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin in Perlis said they were glad to celebrate in the city while undergoing their internship.
"The celebrations here are different from what we have in our hometown in Kano in terms of food, culture and clothing.
"The only common thing is the Hari Raya prayers. Back home, we usually spend time with our families. But here, everyone comes out to take pictures, creating a lively and festive atmosphere," they said.
Assistant engineer Mohd Asri Md Daud and his family from Kedah chose to celebrate in the city to spend time with his in-laws and his daughter-in-law's family.
Carrying his months-old grandchild in a stroller was no obstacle for the family as they gathered for a photo.
"This is our first time coming here to admire the building and take photographs for memories. It will be something we can look back on for our grandchild," he said.
Meanwhile, Bangladeshi student Akter Faruqe and his college mates toured the building before heading to his aunt's house to savour a traditional dessert, shemai.
Akter, dressed in a baju Melayu, said he wanted to help his classmates Sadi Chowdhury and Md Noyon Ahmed ease their homesickness.
"They miss shemai and my aunt will make it so they can feel at home and not miss their families so much during the festivities," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke tells Guan Eng, Chow to end public spat over quit rent
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: DAP has ordered former chief minister Lim Guan Eng and current chief minister Chow Kon Yeow to end their ongoing public spat over Penang's controversial quit rent hikes.
Its secretary- general, Anthony Loke, stressed that party discipline must be upheld at all times and he would convene a meeting involving both leaders to resolve the matter amicably.
"Our focus must remain on serving the people and safeguarding the public interest.
"Party leaders must refrain from public bickering and instead work together constructively in the best interests of the rakyat," Loke said today.
The directive comes as open exchanges between Lim and Chow showed no signs of abating.
Earlier today, Lim intensified his criticism, claiming voters would have withheld support for the state leadership had they known the 2023 election manifesto allowed for such steep tax increases.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/03/1401296/guan-eng-chow-fresh-spat-over-penang-quit-rent-hikes
Loke said the party's central leadership is deeply concerned and disturbed by the ongoing public spat.
"This is an unhealthy and inappropriate way to address internal differences on public policy matters, particularly at a time when the nation is facing external geopolitical and economic uncertainties," he added.
Loke said Lim, as the former secretary-general and former chief minister, should fully appreciate the importance of party discipline.
He said any disagreements, especially from backbenchers on state government decisions, should be addressed through established internal mechanisms rather than aired publicly.
"Differences of opinion should not be expressed in a manner that undermines our own chief minister and the state government.
"At the same time, while Lim's approach is highly inappropriate, Chow, as chief minister, should take serious note of the concerns raised, as they involve public interest and reflect grievances from various stakeholders.
"While there is a need to review quit rent rates in commercial zones to ensure the financial sustainability of the state government, the approach must be balanced, responsive, and considerate," he stressed.
In particular, Loke said, quit rent involving educational institutions, such as public schools, should not be increased excessively, and that a nominal rate should be applied.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan RTD denies high-speed chase led to fatal Tanah Merah crash
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Road Transport Department (RTD) has refuted social media allegations that its enforcement officers were involved in a high-speed chase that led to a fatal road accident in Tanah Merah yesterday.
State RTD director Mohd Misuari Abdullah described the claims, which circulated via a viral 34-second video, as "untrue and baseless".
He said the department's vehicle involved in the crash at Km85.5 of Jalan Kota Baru–Machang–Jeli near Kampung Rahmat, was conducting a routine patrol under Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026 at the time of the incident.
"During the operation, the RTD officers had adhered to all standard operating procedures and traffic regulations.
"Any allegation that the accident was caused by them pursuing a lorry is inaccurate and should not be spread without verification," he said.
Mohd Misuari stressed that further investigation into the incident falls under the jurisdiction of the police and urged the public to refrain from speculation or sharing unverified information until investigations are completed.
He also conveyed condolences to the family of the deceased and expressed hope for the swift recovery of those injured.
"RTD remains committed to carrying out its enforcement duties professionally, with integrity and prudence, to ensure the safety of all road users," he added.
It was reported that the son of an Astro Awani senior editor died in a road crash involving an RTD patrol vehicle at Km85.5 Jalan Kota Baru-Machang-Jeli near Kampung Rahmat, Bukit Bunga yesterday.
The victim, identified as Mursyid Azmi, 25, was driving a Perodua Myvi when the collision occurred.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 10-year-old boy becomes Sarawak's latest rabies fatality
Article:
KUCHING: A 10-year-old boy is the latest fatality from rabies in Sarawak, bringing the state's total number of cases this year to three.
The victim died at the Sarawak General Hospital (SGH) on March 18.
The Sarawak Health Department, in a statement today, said the boy had a history of being bitten by a stray dog on Feb 11.
However, he only washed the wound and did not seek further medical treatment at the time.
"The victim began showing symptoms such as loss of appetite, vomiting, and hydrophobia on March 11.
"He was taken to a clinic on March 13 before being referred to SGH on the same day," the statement said.
Laboratory tests later confirmed the rabies infection.
To date, 93 human rabies cases have been detected in Sarawak since the outbreak was declared in July 2017, with 86 of those cases resulting in death.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nationwide celebration as Muslims perform Aidilfitri prayers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muslims across Malaysia welcomed the arrival of Syawal today, performing Aidilfitri prayers at mosques and surau nationwide in an atmosphere of gratitude and festivity.
From the National Mosque in the capital to rural prayer halls, congregants took advantage of the fair weather, gathering as early as 7am to recite the takbir before the morning prayers and sermons.
In the capital, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia, today performed Aidiltiri prayers at the Istana Negara main surau.
[[nid:1401390]]
Also present were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and his wife, Datin Seri Hamidah Khamis, Cabinet ministers, as well as foreign representatives.
In PERAK, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers together with about 2,000 congregants at the Ubudiah Mosque in Kuala Kangsar.
[[nid:1401389]]
After the prayers, His Royal Highness visited the grave of his father, Sultan Azlan Shah, at the Royal Mausoleum.
In KEDAH, the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers at the Zahir Mosque in Alor Star together with more than 2,000 congregants.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor, as well as members of the State Executive Council, were also in attendance.
In PERLIS, the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, performed the Aidilfitri prayers with more than 2,000 congregants at the Dataran Istiadat Istana Arau, with the prayers led by state Grand Imam Amnan Wuthqa Azanol.
Also in attendance were the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, together with their children.
Menteri Besar of Perlis Abu Bakar Hamzah was also present.
In TERENGGANU, the Sultan of Terengganu, Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin and the Sultanah of Terengganu, Sultanah Nur Zahirah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers with more than 2,500 congregants.
[[nid:1401388]]
Also present was Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, with the prayers led by state Mufti Datuk Mohamad Sabri Harun at the Al-Muktafi Billah Shah Mosque, Kuala Terengganu.
In PAHANG, the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, and the Tengku Ampuan of Pahang, Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers at the Sultan Ahmad 1 State Mosque in Kuantan.
Their Royal Highnesses were accompanied by their son, the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, as well as their other children.
Menteri Besar of Pahang Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail was also present.
In SELANGOR, the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, accompanied by the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, performed the Aidilfitri prayers at the Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque.
[[nid:1401387]]
In NEGRI SEMBILAN, the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, and the Tunku Ampuan Besar, Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, arrived at about 8.28am at the Tuanku Munawir Royal Mosque in Kuala Pilah to perform the Aidilfitri prayers together with more than 1,000 congregants.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun was also in attendance.
After the prayers, which were led by Imam Abdul Rahman Ibrahim, Their Royal Highnesses spent time greeting the congregants before departing for Istana Besar Seri Menanti to attend the Jamuan Rakyat.
In JOHOR, the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, performed the Aidilfitri prayers at the Sultan Abu Bakar State Mosque in Johor Bahru together with more than 2,000 congregants this morning.
[[nid:1401386]]
The Aidilfitri prayers, which were also attended by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, were led by state Grand Imam Muhamad Bukharuddin Ismail.
In KELANTAN, the Tengku Mahkota of Kelantan, Tengku Muhammad Fakhry Petra, performed the Aidilfitri prayers with thousands of congregants at Muhammadi Jamek Mosque in Kota Bharu.
Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz were in attendance.
In PENANG, Governor Tun Ramli Ngah Talib and his wife, Toh Puan Raja Noora Ashikin Raja Abdullah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers together with about 5,000 Muslims at Al Bukhary Mosque in George Town.
In MELAKA, Governor Tun Mohd Ali Rustam and his wife, Toh Puan Asmah Abdul Rahman, joined about 3,000 congregants in performing the Aidilfitri prayers at the Al-Azim State Mosque in Bukit Palah.
In SABAH, Governor Tun Musa Aman, together with Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, performed the Aidilfitri prayers with thousands of congregants at the Sabah State Mosque, led by State Mosque Grand Imam Suhaidi Haji Kuanting.
Meanwhile, in SARAWAK, Governor Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar, and Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg joined about 5,000 congregants in performing the Aidilfitri prayers at the State Jamek Mosque in Petra Jaya, before being scheduled to host the Aidilfitri Open House at the Astana Negeri Sarawak from 9am until 4pm.
In PUTRAJAYA, more than 3,000 congregants from around the federal government administrative centre began arriving at the Tuanku Mizan Zainal Abidin Mosque (MTMZA) as early as 7.30am to perform the Aidilfitri prayers.
A check by Bernama found that the MTMZA, also known as the Iron Mosque, was also a focal point for foreigners, especially students from universities around Putrajaya.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka police bust online sex scam targeting Taiwanese victims
Article:
MELAKA: Police have crippled an online sex scam syndicate following the arrest of 15 individuals, including 13 Chinese nationals, during a raid at a terrace house in Kampung Lapan, Tengkera.
Melaka Tengah district police chief Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the suspects, aged between 26 and 49, were detained at 6pm last Thursday.
The group, which includes two Malaysian men and a Chinese woman, is believed to have operated a call centre targeting victims in Taiwan with non-existent sexual services.
"Investigations showed that the 13 Chinese nationals entered the country using social visit passes and have been operating in Melaka since early March," he told a press conference at the district police headquarters today.
Police seized equipment valued at RM85,000, including 13 computers, 40 mobile phones and two passports.
All suspects have been remanded for four days until Monday to assist investigations under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating, as well as Sections 6(1)(c) and 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
"Police advise the public to remain vigilant against any services offered online," Patit said.
Information regarding online scams can be channelled to the Commercial Crime Investigation Department hotline at 011-2111222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: West Coast Expressway records sharp traffic increase during Raya period
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The West Coast Expressway (WCE) recorded a surge in traffic, with between 160,000 and 170,000 vehicles using the highway daily throughout the Aidilfitri festive period.
During the two peak days of the holidays, the highway operated by West Coast Expressway Holdings Berhad (WCEHB) saw an average daily traffic volume exceeding 200,000 vehicles across its completed sections.
WCE general manager of strategic communications and stakeholder engagement Syed Mohammed Idid said the increase reflected high travel demand during the long weekend.
"On Thursday and Friday, there was a surge in vehicles on the WCE even though sections 3, 4 and 7 in Selangor are not yet open. This shows that road users increasingly view the WCE as an efficient route," he said.
Syed Mohammed added that WCE remained committed to enhancing safety and travel efficiency for all users.
The highway operator advised motorists to plan their journeys early to avoid peak congestion, which is expected from the afternoon to night.
"Highway users are encouraged to download the WCE app for real-time traffic updates and live CCTV feeds. We also advise departing early in the morning to avoid peak hours and ensuring vehicles are roadworthy, with sufficient fuel and functioning tyres, brakes and lights," he said.
He reminded drivers to adhere to speed limits, wear seatbelts and remain alert. In the event of an emergency, users are advised to wait behind the guardrails for assistance from WCE patrols, which operate 24/7.
The WCE toll-free helpline at 1700-81-6600 is also available around the clock to assist road users.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prasarana submits recovery plan to ministry after LRT Kelana Jaya line disruptions [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prasarana Malaysia Berhad (Prasarana) has submitted a recovery action plan to Transport Minister Anthony Loke following recurring service disruptions on the LRT Kelana Jaya line last month.
Prasarana president and chief executive officer Amir Hamdan said the report, which details technical measures and customer experience improvements, was presented during a post-cabinet meeting approximately three weeks ago.
"We provided an explanation and reported to the post-cabinet meeting. This included specific technical actions and customer experience strategies. The report was well-received.
"Our priority now is ensuring the action plan is executed within the stipulated timeframe to prevent a recurrence, or at least to minimise risks," he told a press conference at the Pasar Seni LRT station today.
Last month, the Kelana Jaya line reportedly suffered major disruptions, leading to overcrowding at several major hubs, including the Sri Rampai, Pasar Seni, Masjid Jamek and KLCC stations.
Amir earlier conducted an operational survey of Rapid KL rail and bus services, beginning at the Ampang Park MRT station and visiting the Tun Razak Exchange (TRX) MRT hub before heading to Pasar Seni.
On investigations by the Land Public Transport Agency (Apad), Amir said the agency is briefed on every service disruption according to standard procedures.
"Reports to Apad are made periodically within 24 hours, seven days, and 21 days following an incident. They are constantly updated on every disruption," he said.
Meanwhile, Prasarana expects public transport ridership in the capital to drop by 50 per cent during the Aidilfitri season as city dwellers return to their hometowns.
Amir said this would not affect operations, with 3,200 personnel deployed throughout the festive period to ensure smooth service.
"Our survey today found operations to be orderly. Passengers have a more comfortable experience with available seating and calm journeys," he said.
Despite the drop in passengers, Prasarana will maintain off-peak train frequencies: seven minutes for the Kelana Jaya line and 10 minutes for MRT services. Peak-hour operations will resume on Tuesday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tiong calls for urgent talks over diesel price impact on tourism
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing has instructed his ministry to initiate immediate consultations with the Finance Ministry over the impact of rising diesel prices on the tourism transport sector.
In a statement, Tiong said his ministry is closely and carefully monitoring the recent increase in diesel prices and understands the challenges faced by operators, particularly the sudden rise in operating costs for those heavily dependent on diesel.
"If this matter is not handled properly, it could have a ripple effect on inbound tourism, businesses, and the overall stability of the tourism ecosystem.
"I have received feedback from associations and relevant stakeholders and have instructed the ministry to immediately initiate consultations with the Finance Ministry.
"These discussions will focus on the current situation and consider targeted measures, including temporary, time-bound support to help ease short-term pressures arising from the current situation, partly influenced by developments in the Middle East," he said.
Recognising that the tourism transport sector is a key pillar of the national tourism industry, Tiong said while addressing current pressures, the government must also consider the long-term health of this sector.
"Beyond cost issues, it is equally important to continue strengthening safety standards, vehicle performance, and the quality of services provided to visitors.
"In this regard, any measures taken will be guided by an approach to stabilise and upgrade.
"Our goal is to help operators navigate this challenging period while encouraging gradual improvements within the sector, including vehicle renewal, appropriate financial support, and overall enhancements to the regulatory framework.
"I would like to emphasise that the government's objective is not merely to mitigate the immediate impact, but to build a more resilient and competitive tourism transport system.
"Any form of support introduced will be designed with the industry's long-term development in mind, rather than as temporary fixes or one-off solutions," he said.
Tiong urged industry players to remain engaged in constructive dialogue and cooperate to stabilise the sector ahead of Visit Malaysia Year 2026.
He called on all parties to remain calm, act prudently, and not be driven purely by emotion.
"The government will do its utmost to balance the various interests involved and seek reasonable, compromise-based solutions.
"While the measures introduced may not fully satisfy all parties, we will continue striving to implement the most appropriate arrangements, taking into account overall feasibility and the long-term interests of the industry," he said.
As of March 18, the price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia has risen to 80 sen to RM4.71 per litre, while in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, it remained at RM2.15 per litre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran's new Supreme Leader absent on Persian New Year as speech read out on TV
Article:
TEHRAN: People in Iran eagerly awaited a first public appearance by the country's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, on Persian New Year, or Nowruz, on Friday - but a speech attributed to him was instead read out on state television.
On the screens, a still image of the supreme leader was shown alongside an Iranian flag, reported German news agency dpa.
Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared in public or released audio or video messages since his appointment on March 8, leading to speculation about his health.
He succeeded his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in an Israeli airstrike on Tehran on the first day of the US-Israeli attack on Feb 28.
Mojtaba Khamenei also lost his wife, mother and a brother-in-law in the bombings.
According to the US government, Mojtaba Khamenei is injured. No further details are available.
Nowruz, meaning "new day," is an ancestral festivity marking the first day of spring and the renewal of nature. It marks the spring equinox in the northern hemisphere, when night and day are of equal length, and falls on March 20 or 21.
In his Nowruz statement, Khamenei thanked the president, the government, the armed forces and the Iranian people for their resistance against the joint US-Israeli attacks.
"The adversaries were not relying solely on military means such as missiles, drones and torpedoes", he said, adding that Iran's "front line" was broader than its enemies wished to acknowledge.
Khamenei declared the new Persian year the "year of the resistance economy in the spirit of national unity and security."
The supreme leader's message also urged all media outlets to exercise restraint and avoid highlighting vulnerabilities, warning that doing otherwise could help the enemy achieve its objectives.
He also denied that Iranian forces had carried out attacks on Turkey and Oman.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Towards a more caring, united and resilient society
Article:
I would like to extend my warmest wishes of Selamat Hari Raya Aidilfitri, Maaf Zahir and Batin to all Muslims in Malaysia.
This year's Aidilfitri is observed in a spirit of reflection, as the world continues to grapple with ongoing conflict in the Middle East, alongside many global challenges that are affecting lives everywhere.
Here at home, Malaysians are also facing rising living costs and economic uncertainties that demand resilience, prudence, and unity.
In these challenging times, I wish to call upon our Muslim friends to continue embracing the true values of Aidilfitri — moderation, gratitude, compassion, and care for those in need.
Let us celebrate with simplicity and avoid excess, in line with the spirit of Ramadan which teaches sacrifice, discipline, and empathy.
At the same time, let us strengthen the bonds of unity and solidarity, extending a helping hand to one another regardless of background.
As many families navigate financial pressures, it is important to practise prudent spending and responsible financial management for the wellbeing of both households and the wider community.
May this Aidilfitri serve as a renewed beginning for all of us to reaffirm our shared values, strengthen our humanity, and work together towards building a more caring, united, and resilient society.
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
National Unity Advocate
Kuala Lumpur
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King hosts Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, the King and Queen of Malaysia, today graced the Hari Raya Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara here.
The event was attended by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, other cabinet ministers and representatives of foreign diplomatic missions, including those from the United Kingdom, Iran, Azerbaijan and Indonesia.
Among the more than 600 guests were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, and Malaysian National News Agency chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai and editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj.
Among the dishes served were Johor specialities such as kacang pool and sup tulang merah, as well as lemang, ketupat, rendang and nasi beriani.
The event was enlivened by Hari Raya songs performed by the police Quintet Band, as well as a special performance by singer and Yayasan Kebajikan Artis Tanahair chairman Datuk DJ Dave.
Their Majesties also took time to mingle with the guests.
Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim and Raja Zarith Sofiah attended Aidilfitri prayers at the Istana Negara Main Surau.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US allows 30-day sale of Iran oil at sea in bid to tame prices
Article:
WASHINGTON: The Trump administration has issued a 30-day sanctions waiver allowing the sale of Iranian oil currently at sea, a strategic move aimed at cooling global energy prices amid the ongoing conflict with Tehran.
The waiver, announced on Friday by the US Treasury Department, permits the purchase of approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian oil.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the move is designed to relieve pressure on energy supplies and "use Iranian barrels against Tehran" to keep prices down during the military campaign, dubbed Operation Epic Fury.
Global oil prices have surged by about 50% since the US and Israel launched strikes on Feb 28.
The White House is increasingly concerned that high energy costs will hurt businesses and consumers ahead of the November midterm elections.
Under the licence, which remains in effect until April 19, Iranian oil may be imported into the US if necessary to complete a sale or delivery, though it remains unclear if any will reach American soil.
The move is primarily expected to benefit China, the top buyer of Iranian crude.
Running out of options
The waiver is the third time in two weeks the Treasury has eased sanctions on adversaries to tame a market that has seen prices soar above US$100 a barrel.
Previous waivers were issued for Russian oil stranded at sea, including a specific 30-day licence on March 5 for India to purchase Russian supplies.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said these supplies could reach Asian markets within three to four days and be refined over the next six weeks.
However, analysts remain sceptical of the long-term impact. Brett Erickson of Obsidian Risk Advisors said price control efforts may be limited until the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.
The strait, a conduit for 20% of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas, was effectively closed by Iran following the outbreak of hostilities.
"The easing of sanctions raises concerns about the rapid depletion of Washington's economic toolkit.
If we've reached the point of loosening sanctions on the country we are at war with, we're really running out of options," Erickson said.
Domestic measures
In a further attempt to stabilise costs, the administration on Wednesday announced a 60-day waiver of the Jones Act shipping law.
This temporarily allows foreign-flagged vessels to move fuel, fertiliser, and other goods between US ports.
Despite the temporary easing, the administration maintains it is keeping "maximum pressure" on Iran's ability to access the international financial system.
Mark Dubowitz, CEO of the Foundation for the Defence of Democracies, said on X that the decision was a "smart move" to help win the fight against the regime.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aidilfitri tragedy as fire guts three terrace houses in Kota Baru
Article:
KOTA BARU: Hari Raya Aidilfitri turned tragic for three families today when their double-storey terrace houses were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Bunut Payong here this morning.
The 8.27am blaze left two units completely gutted, while a third was 80 per cent destroyed.
A Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department spokesperson said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 8.27am.
Fire engines from the Kota Baru and Tunjung stations, supported by a water tanker from Pengkalan Chepa, were dispatched to the scene.
"Upon arrival, the operations team found three double-storey terrace houses on fire.
"Two units were 100 per cent destroyed, while another was 80 per cent burnt. No casualties were reported," the spokesperson said, adding that firefighting operations are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: One colour, one heart: 52 family members coordinate for Raya
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: For the past eight years, the family of Muda Abdullah and Khatijah Said has turned heads every Hari Raya with their tradition of wearing matching festive attire.
Muda, 68, and Khatijah, 62, view the coordinated outfits as more than just a style choice; it is a symbol of the togetherness they have built within their large family.
Since 2018, the family has rotated through various themes, including emerald green, navy blue, purple, sage green, soft grey, maroon, royal blue and this year's choice, brick orange.
The selection process is democratic, with the family of 52 — comprising 12 children, 10 in-laws and 30 grandchildren — voting on three proposed colours to ensure everyone is satisfied with the final decision.
"When we vote, everyone is happy. No one feels left out. While the colour is the same, they can choose their own patterns, though we prefer plain designs to keep it looking neat and harmonious," Muda said at his home in Kampung Tok Dir today.
Muda admitted that coordinating such a large group is no small feat, but said the sight of his children and grandchildren in unison makes the effort worthwhile.
"When I see everyone in the same colour, it feels like we are of one heart. It is wonderful to see the compatibility and harmony among the children, in-laws and grandchildren," he said.
Despite the significant expenses involved in preparing outfits and providing duit raya for such a large brood, Muda does not view it as a burden.
"It costs a bit of money, but that is fine. The satisfaction comes from seeing the family happy. On Raya morning, seeing everyone laughing and smiling makes any problem disappear," he added.
Beyond the fashion tradition, the family has maintained another staple for over 20 years: a signature lamb gulai, cooked together every year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Haiyaa: Uncle Roger to 'slim down' operations after announcing outlet closure
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Restaurant chain "Fuiyoh! It's Uncle Roger" says it will be "slimming down" its operations after announcing the closure of its branch at the IPC Shopping Centre.
The company said that when it started one-and-a-half years ago, it grew too quickly and signed leases in locations that were not the right fit in a rush to capitalise on early momentum.
"Instead of expansion, we are now slimming down our operations and running a lean team, something we realised we should've done from the start."
The chain, known for its fried rice, also said it was reworking the menu to include more protein and sides to ensure customers feel they are getting better value for money.
"All full-time employees have received severance, and we wish them the best with what comes next," it said, adding that its outlets in KL, Selangor, Johor and KLIA Terminal 2 remain operational.
In 2024, Malaysian comedian Nigel Ng, better known as "Uncle Roger" announced he would open his
first restaurant
in Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran fires missiles at joint US-UK base in Indian Ocean
Article:
WASHINGTON: Iran recently fired two ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base on Diego Garcia, suggesting Tehran possesses a longer strike range than previously estimated.
Iran fired two ballistic missiles toward the joint US-UK military base at Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, according to a report by The Wall Street Journal on Friday citing US officials.
Neither missile hit the target, which is about 4,000km from Iranian territory. However, the report said the launch suggests Tehran has missiles with longer ranges than previously thought.
One missile failed in flight, while the other was targeted by an interceptor fired from a US warship, though it was unclear if the missile was hit. The Pentagon did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Diego Garcia, located in the Chagos Islands, is one of two bases Britain allows the United States to use for operations regarding Iran. American forces station bombers and other equipment at the base, which serves as a key hub for operations in Asia, including previous bombing campaigns in Afghanistan and Iraq.
Britain has agreed to hand the Chagos Islands back to Mauritius after holding the archipelago since the 1960s. It maintains a lease for the base on Diego Garcia, the largest of the islands.
US President Donald Trump has criticised the decision.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kampung Batu Enam skies light up in 40-year Raya eve tradition
Article:
KUALA NERUS: For more than 40 years, the skies over Kampung Batu Enam have been illuminated by a spectacular fireworks display to mark the eve of Hari Raya Aidilfitri.
The annual tradition sees crowds and residents gather at the Batu Enam roundabout to witness a display that typically lasts for over an hour.
Resident Ismail Harun, 40, said the scale of the activity depends on the supply of fireworks pooled by the community.
"The fireworks display in this village has become a major attraction on the eve of Aidilfitri.
"Usually, the roads leading to the village roundabout will be congested with visitors eager to see the various types of fireworks set off by residents," he said.
Ismail added that the activity is a casual affair that serves as a reunion for those returning to their hometown for the festivities.
Visitor Muhammad Ismadi Alias, 38, said the village's reputation for its fireworks tradition is well-known.
"Various types of fireworks are set off here, drawing large crowds. It is only on the eve of Aidilfitri that I get to see so many fireworks being played at once," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NST Leader : Making balik kampung safer
Article:
THE great Malaysian balik kampung ritual to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri is also an uncomfortable paradox.
It's a fun but stressful time to journey back home in all parts of the nation but it's also a precarious trip fraught with traffic mishaps, some deadly.
While the odds of getting into a traffic altercation is dire at 2,000 accidents in a single day, the data states that 1 in 81,300 travellers per trip will be killed, based on 2025 festive season data.
This is what happens on the period: the North-South Expressway traffic is traversed by 2.3 million vehicles daily.
Traffic police statistics compiled from their Ops Selamat operations between 2022 and 2025 reaffirms: about 31,000 accidents, 461 of them fatal, causing 495 deaths, including 211 motorcyclists.
The only positive aspect? Total accidents dropped from 12,407 to 9,859 in 2025.
Deaths dropped from 140 to 77, thanks to vigilance and safety awareness by authorities and motorists alike.
The first day of Aidilfitri is always the deadliest: in 2022, after pandemic restrictions were lifted, 28 deaths were recorded, 19 of them motorcyclists.
The leading causes were negligence, loss of control, entering the opposite lane and fatigue.
Accidents have been conscientiously mitigated: infrastructure has vastly improved: known black spots widened, brighter markings on stretches rife with head-on collisions, better lighting at rest and recreations entries and exits, where drowsy-driver accidents cluster and better signage on last-mile rural roads.
Thousands of police officers supervise roadblocks, rest areas and high-risk stretches to enforce speed limits and monitor fatigue.
PLUS' smart highways that utilises dynamic digital signboard messaging to adjust speed limits in real time based on traffic density and weather has reduced collisions during peak congestion.
Since fatigue and drowsiness caused 20 fatal crashes in 2023, PLUS expanded the R & R pitstops to include dedicated sleeping zones.
The Malaysian Institute of Road Safety Research (MIROS) has a sound advice: drivers should stop every two hours regardless of alert levels.
Travellers should avoid departing on the day before Raya eve and Raya eve itself but leave three to four days earlier.
Work commitments render this strategy impractical but data shows that accidents spike highly on these prescribed Raya period.
Perhaps the authorities could give tax breaks or more subsidies to train and bus operators to entice motorists to become commuters, just for the Raya season.
The 2025 Op Selamat drop of 41 per cent in fatal crashes, despite more accidents, is the outcome of better enforcement, more free rail/bus alternatives and effective public communication on departure timing.
The balik kampung ritual is revered but lives of loved ones are always at stake despite safer driving and riding initiatives.
It's up to the motorists to take heed to avoid becoming the next tragic statistic.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iconic Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad ready for Raya family photos, says Hannah Yeoh [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has invited Muslims celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri today to visit the newly revitalised Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad for family photos.
In a Facebook post wishing Malaysians "Selamat Hari Raya", Yeoh highlighted the building's recent upgrades.
"If you have time, stop by Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad in Kuala Lumpur with your family. The photos would be pretty as the building has been upgraded," she said.
Block One of the Sultan Abdul Samad Building Complex opened on Feb 2, marking the first completed phase of Khazanah Nasional Bhd's Dana Warisan restoration initiative.
Restoration works focus on safeguarding the building's character as a national heritage site while enhancing safety, accessibility and the overall visitor experience.
Visitors can now enjoy a range of offerings at
Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad
, including immersive exhibition spaces and galleries, a one-stop visitor centre, experiential retail and craft-making spaces, as well as lettable event halls and venues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah-based journalists find new meaning in Raya away from home
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri away from family for the first time has proven to be a meaningful experience for Kuala Lumpur native Syed Iylia Hariz Al Qadri Syedn Izman, who is marking the occasion in Sabah this year.
The 37-year-old Bernama journalist moved to the state in October last year and decided to remain for the festivities to experience the local atmosphere.
"This year I decided to celebrate Raya here because I wanted to experience the Hari Raya atmosphere in Sabah.
"My in-laws are also coming to celebrate with me here, so it gives a more special meaning even though I am far from my hometown," he said.
Syed Iylia, whose grandparents are in Kuala Lumpur and his wife is from Perak, said he misses the tradition of gathering on the first day of Raya.
"Of course, I miss seeking forgiveness in the morning and enjoying traditional dishes together. I try to recreate that by connecting online with family, preparing simple festive dishes and decorating my home," he said, describing Sabah's celebrations as relaxed and culturally diverse.
He said he tried to create the festive atmosphere by connecting online with his family, preparing simple festive dishes and decorating his home.
On the differences in celebration, he described Sabah's Raya celebrations as more relaxed and culturally diverse.
For Penang-born Zacherlyn @ Noor Zafira, a reporter with a local daily who has been in Sabah for 16 years, the high cost of festive air travel is a major hurdle to returning home.
However, her situation is also marked by loss.
[[nid:1401306]]
"I no longer have anyone as all my family members have passed away since 2022, and that was the last year I returned home for Hari Raya," she said.
Despite this, she said what she misses most are the moments spent with the loved ones who raised her.
"What I miss are the moments with my late grandfather, grandmother and aunts; the food they prepared, the happiness at home and the laughter we shared.
"These memories remain close to my heart as strength while I continue building my own life," she said.
To maintain the festive mood, Zacherlyn keeps things simple by buying Raya cookies, cleaning her home while listening to Raya songs, decorating with ketupat replicas and watching festive shows.
She also attends open houses when invited and occasionally revisits old family photos to relive past celebrations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ten killed, four missing in South Korea car plant fire
Article:
DAEJEON: Ten people were killed and four remain missing after a massive fire broke out at a car parts plant in Daejeon on Friday.
The blaze, which started at about 1.17pm, left 59 others injured. Authorities said nine of the bodies were recovered from the third floor of the factory, while one was found on the second floor.
A total of 170 workers were inside the building when the fire began. Search operations were delayed until 10.50pm on Friday as experts assessed the structural integrity of the plant and managed 200kg of sodium, which posed an explosion risk.
Firefighters are currently using rescue dogs and tearing down internal structures to locate the four missing people.
"We plan to deploy rescue workers and continue the search for those missing," an official said.
-BERNAMA-YONHAP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Guan Eng, Chow in fresh spat over Penang quit rent hikes
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The open exchanges between Bagan member of parliament Lim Guan Eng and Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow over Penang's quit rent hikes show no signs of abating.
Lim has intensified his criticism, claiming that many voters would not have supported the state leadership if they had known the 2023 state election manifesto allowed for such steep increases in quit rent rates.
He expressed shock at Chow's recent remarks that an election manifesto is not a binding document and does not restrict the state from introducing new policies.
Lim said this includes significant quit tax hikes deemed necessary for economic stability.
Lim stressed that the "Kita Penang, Kita Boleh" unity manifesto launched ahead of the last state polls did not indicate that the quit rent hikes could be increased by thousands of times.
"There was no indication to us as candidates then that the manifesto allowed such drastic hikes.
"Many voters would not have supported the chief minister if they had known this," he said in his social media posting.
In his recent press conference, Chow addressed criticism that the state government's initiatives were not part of its election manifesto, saying such arguments were misplaced.
"The manifesto outlines what a party commits to when contesting elections, and we report progress on those commitments annually in the state assembly," he said.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/03/1400321/chow-rebuts-claims-being-out-touch-community
Chow cited the establishment of the Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC) as an example, noting that it was not included in the 15th state election manifesto, but was still implemented due to current economic needs.
Lim urged the state government to honour its manifesto pledge to ensure that no group is marginalised and that development benefits all segments of society.
Describing the increases as excessive, he cited several cases involving sharp hikes affecting landowners across the state, including in Batu Kawan.
He said some landowners experienced increases running into hundreds or even thousands of times, causing distress and financial strain.
Lim also claimed his office had received numerous complaints, including from a landowner in Simpang Empat, who is struggling with declining business and loan commitments following a sharp rise in the quit rent hike.
He questioned Chow's assertion that he had not received many complaints during his engagements statewide, asking whether the chief minister had sufficiently met affected constituents in Batu Kawan.
Lim further criticised the state government for defending the accuracy of the quit rent calculations, noting that several cases saw reductions of between 80 and 90 per cent after being raised.
"If the system is correct, why are there such significant reductions? This shows the calculation system is flawed and needs to be reviewed," he said.
Lim has maintained a steady stream of criticism against Chow since the new quit rent rates took effect at the beginning of the year.
He called for a reassessment of the quit rent rates, urging the state government to consult stakeholders and introduce a fairer and more reasonable mechanism.
Lim added that the government should prioritise the people in line with its principles.
Chow recently dismissed claims that he was out of touch with grassroots concerns, stressing that his frequent statewide engagements, including the Jelajah Ramadan programme, demonstrated otherwise.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man injured in Kulim after suspect fires revolver during late‑night dispute
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — A 24‑year‑old factory operator sustained gunshot injuries to his arm and chest after an argument turned violent in Padang Serai late last night.
The incident, reported by the
New Straits Times
, happened at 11.50pm outside a house in Kampung Permatang Durian, Seberang Paya.
Kulim deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the confrontation began when the victim was stopped by a 42‑year‑old man who had earlier asked him to come to his home in Kampung Naga Lilit.
“The suspect then fired a single shot from a revolver, hitting the victim in the right arm and chest,” he said when contacted.
The victim was taken to Kulim Hospital for treatment.
Police later arrested the suspect, a lorry driver, in front of his house in Kampung Naga Lilit.
A revolver, 10 rounds of live ammunition, two spent casings and a Honda Civic believed to be linked to the shooting were seized during the arrest.
Investigations showed that both the suspect and the victim have prior criminal histories.
Police said the suspect has 11 previous criminal cases and three drug‑related offences, while the victim has six narcotics cases and several other criminal entries.
The case is being investigated under Section 3 of the Firearms (Increased Penalties) Act 1971 and Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.

Title: Sarawak records third rabies death of the year after 10‑year‑old Kuching boy bitten by stray dog
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — A 10‑year‑old boy from Kuching has died of rabies, marking Sarawak’s third fatality from the disease this year.
The Sarawak Health Department said that the child had been bitten by a stray dog on February 11 but did not receive medical attention after the incident, as reported by
Free Malaysia Today.
The department said the boy had cleaned the wound himself at home, which meant he did not receive the immediate medical care required after a suspected rabies exposure.
“The victim was brought to a clinic on March 13 before being referred to Sarawak General Hospital and being admitted to intensive care on the same day,” the department said in a statement.
It said laboratory tests confirmed rabies infection on March 14 and that the boy died four days later on March 18.
According to the department, the boy began showing symptoms such as vomiting, loss of appetite, stiff neck and hydrophobia on March 11.
It said the delay in seeking treatment after the dog bite was the main contributing factor in the case.
The department reminded the public that rabies can spread through bites, scratches or contact with the saliva of infected animals, including dogs and cats.
It advised that any bite or scratch wound should be washed under running water with soap for at least 15 minutes before seeking immediate medical care.
The department also urged the public not to feed stray animals, avoid handling carcasses of animals that died without known causes, and ensure pets receive annual rabies vaccinations.
It said 87 people have died from rabies in Sarawak since the outbreak was declared in July 2017.

Title: Kelantan JPJ denies role in fatal crash involving journalist’s son, says viral video of lorry chase is false
Article:
KOTA BHARU, March 21 — The Kelantan Road Transport Department (JPJ) has refuted viral social media allegations linking one of its enforcement vehicles to a fatal accident at KM 85.5 of Jalan Kota Bharu-Machang-Jeli near Kampung Rahmat, Bukit Bunga, yesterday.
Kelantan JPJ director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said the department takes the spread of inaccurate information regarding the incident seriously.
“The claim that the accident was caused by JPJ personnel chasing a lorry, as depicted in a 34-second viral video, is false and baseless.
“The departmental vehicle was conducting an official patrol at the time in conjunction with Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026,” he said in a statement today.
He added that the personnel involved adhered to standard operating procedures (SOPs) and traffic regulations while carrying out their duties.
Mohd Misuari added that further investigations fall under the jurisdiction of the police and urged the public to refrain from speculation or the dissemination of unverified information until the probe is completed.
“We also wish to express our deepest sympathy to the victims involved and offer our condolences to the family of the deceased. We pray that his soul be blessed and placed among the righteous,” he said.
In the 4.30pm incident yesterday, Mursyid Azmi, 25, the son of Astro Awani senior editor Husna Yusop, was killed following a collision involving two vehicles.
Initial reports indicate that the Perodua Myvi driven by the victim, who was travelling from Ipoh, Perak, towards Rantau Panjang, had veered into the opposite lane before colliding with the right side of a JPJ Toyota Fortuner. — Bernama

Title: International Forest Day 2026: Malaysia cuts logging limit under MP13 to protect forests
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 21 — The government continues to strengthen its commitment to Sustainable Forest Management (SFM) by setting the Annual Allowable Cut (CTT) in Permanent Reserved Forests (HSK) for the 13th Malaysia Plan (MP13) at an area of 890,000 hectares (ha), compared to 1,165,300 ha during the 12th Malaysia Plan (MP12).
Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said that the reduction of CTT is a strategic step to balance economic needs and forest preservation, as well as to strengthen the practice of SFM in the country.
He said that the measure not only has a positive impact on forest conservation efforts but also ensures that the supply of timber to the industry can be maintained continuously in a stable quantity.
In 2025, the forestry and agriculture sectors recorded a contribution of RM6.006 billion to the country's gross domestic product (GDP).
“This simultaneously demonstrates a positive economic impact through sustainable forest management activities that include forest product extraction and logging,” he said in a statement here today in conjunction with International Day of Forests.
Arthur said the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment (NRES) through the Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department, Sabah Forestry Department, Sarawak Forestry Department, and state forestry departments are actively empowering forest-based ecotourism areas that have unique natural characteristics.
He said the initiative is a proactive step to support the state forestry department and state authorities, particularly in reducing dependence on conventional forest exploitation activities in line with efforts to transform the logging sector into more sustainable ecotourism development.
In addition, Arthur said the 81st National Land Council Meeting agreed that the state government should prioritise the use of the Ecological Fiscal Transfer for Biodiversity Conservation (EFT) allocation to finance the financial needs for improving the application and payment process for the HSK Entry Permit for online forest-based ecotourism activities.
“In line with this empowerment effort, the Forest Research Institute Malaysia (FRIM) also plays an active role in raising awareness among rural communities about the importance of conservation and sustainability of nature through training programmes and technical guidance,” he said.
He said that among the communities involved are the Persatuan Sahabat Alam Tampik, Janda Baik (Pahang); Koperasi Usahawan Pelancongan Lembah Tembeling, Jerantut; Koperasi Pelancongan Dabong Bhd (Kelantan); and Koperasi Sahabat Gunung Tebu Terengganu Bhd.
“As a result of this guidance, the local community is now capable of managing and conducting eco-tourism and recreation-based activities and businesses in a more systematic, competitive and sustainable manner, without compromising the forest ecosystem,” he said.
Arthur also said that forests rich in biodiversity not only benefit the environment but also become a valuable heritage that must be preserved for future generations.
“Therefore, continuous commitment to preserving and conserving these assets is crucial so that their sustainability can be enjoyed by all Malaysians, now and forever,” he added.
International Day of Forests is celebrated on March 21 each year to raise public awareness about the importance of forests and to encourage efforts to preserve and conserve forest resources for the well-being of society and the stability of the natural ecosystem.
The theme of this year's celebration is Forests and Economies, which highlights the important role of forests in driving global economic prosperity. — Bernama

Title: Musa Aman and Hajiji Noor attend Aidilfitri prayers at Sabah State Mosque
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 21 — Head of State Tun Musa Aman, accompanied by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, performed Aidilfitri prayers with thousands of congregants at the State Mosque today.
Upon arrival, Musa was received by Hajiji, Speaker Datuk Seri Kadzim M Yahya, Deputy Chief Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun, Local Government and Housing Minister and Main Committee for Islamic Celebrations Chairman Datuk Mohd Arifin Arif, State Secretary Datuk Seri Safar Untong, and Sabah Federal Secretary Datuk Mohd Razali Wahab. — Daily Express

Title: Melaka cops arrest 15 in online sex scam targeting Taiwanese victims
Article:
MELAKA, March 21 — Melaka police have busted an online sex scam syndicate offering non-existent services following the arrest of two Malaysians and 13 Chinese nationals in Kampung Lapan, Tengkera here.
Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit said the suspects, aged between 26 and 49, were detained at about 6 pm last Thursday.
“They are believed to be involved in online fraud activities involving bogus sex services targeting Taiwanese victims through a call centre.
“Investigations showed that the 13 Chinese nationals, who include a woman, entered the country using tourist passes and have been in Melaka since early March,” he told a press conference at the Melaka Tengah district police headquarters here today.
He said police seized various equipment believed to have been used to carry out the scam, namely 13 computers, 40 mobile phones and two passports, with the total value of the seizure estimated at RM85,000.
He said all the suspects have been remanded for four days from yesterday until Monday to assist investigations under Section 420 of the Penal Code, as well as Sections 6(1)© and 15(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
“Police advise the public to remain vigilant against any services offered online,” he said.
Any information related to online scam syndicates can be channelled through the Commercial Crime Investigation Department hotline at 011-2111222. — Bernama

Title: Kota Bharu fire leaves three families displaced after homes destroyed on first day of Aidilfitri
Article:
KOTA BHARU, March 21 — No words could capture the heartbreak felt by housewife Rozmawani Abdullah after nearly 80 per cent of her home was destroyed in a fire at Lorong Rela, Kampung Bunut Payong, here this morning.
Besides Rozmawani’s house, the fire, which broke out at about 8.30 am, also destroyed two other nearby homes.
The 42-year-old woman said that as soon as she realised what was happening, she rushed to get her two children out of the house and ran to a neighbour’s home to save themselves.
“I immediately ran out of the house with my two children — Muhammad Ammar Darwisy Mohd Fadzli, 11, who is a disabled child, and Afiyah Hanan, eight — and we sought safety at a neighbour’s house.
“I didn’t have time to take my husband Mohd Fadzli Ab Rashid’s documents. The only thing I managed to save was our Raya clothes bag, which happened to be in the living room,” she said when contacted by Bernama today.
Recounting the incident further, Rozmawani said that at the time of the fire, her husband and her eldest son, Muhammad Zafran Zarif, 14, had gone to Masjid Bunut Payong to perform the Aidilfitri prayers.
“Thankfully, our neighbour’s son, Muhammad Fitri Mat Nawi, 28, came to help us. He told us the fire started from the neighbouring house, which belongs to a retired civil servant. They were not at home at the time because they had gone to the mosque for the Aidilfitri prayers.
“It is very painful for us because this hardship happened to our family at a time when everyone else is celebrating 1st day of Syawal with joy, but for us, it turned into sorrow when our home was destroyed by fire,” she said, adding that she would spend Aidilfitri at her mother’s house in Kampung Tiong, near here.
Meanwhile, Kota Darulnaim Fire and Rescue Station’s operations commander, Mohd Amin Mohd Kari, said his team received an emergency call at 8.27 a,m prompting 22 firefighters to rush to the scene.
“The fire involved three double-storey terrace houses measuring about 18 feet in width each. Two of the houses were completely destroyed, while another suffered about 80 per cent damage. No casualties were reported.
“The situation was brought under control at 9.44 am, and investigations are still ongoing to determine the cause of the fire and the total losses,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Prasarana submits recovery action plan to Transport Ministry to fix Kelana Jaya LRT disruptions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Prasarana Malaysia Berhad (Prasarana) has submitted a comprehensive recovery action plan to the Transport Ministry following the recent service disruptions on the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) line.
Prasarana Malaysia Berhad Group president and chief executive officer Amir Hamdan said the plan, which focuses on specific action items regarding both the technical aspects of operations and the overall customer experience, was presented during a post-cabinet meeting recently.
“Alhamdulillah, I think they accepted our report. It is now up to us to ensure that we execute the action plans in a timely manner to ensure that it won’t happen again, or at least try to minimise the disruption and the risk of disruptions,” he said.
Amir was speaking to reporters after a site visit to monitor Rapid KL rail and bus operations on the morning of the Aidilfitri celebration at MRT Pasar Seni here today, where he also met with on-duty staff and commuters.
Commenting on the involvement of the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), he emphasised that the agency remains continuously engaged with Prasarana’s operational developments.
Amir explained that Prasarana maintains a thorough and standard reporting schedule with APAD whenever an incident occurs to ensure full transparency and compliance with safety standards.
“In this respect, APAD is always with us. Every time there is an incident or instruction, APAD is immediately informed. We have a frequent reporting system to APAD, which includes 24-hour, seven-day, and 21-day reports. That is the standard procedure,” he added.
Previously, Transport Minister Anthony Loke instructed APAD to conduct a thorough probe into the technical issues affecting the Kelana Jaya Line and required Prasarana to outline a clear recovery roadmap to prevent future occurrences on the nation’s busiest rail line.
In a related development, Amir shared that 3,200 Prasarana staff members are on duty throughout the festive season.
He said while passenger volume in the city is estimated to drop by 50 per cent during the Aidilfitri period, the group remains committed to maintaining high service reliability and safety for all commuters. — Bernama

Title: Over 20,000 gather at Johor MB’s Raya open house in show of ‘Bangsa Johor’ unity
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 21 — Over 20,000 people of various ethnicities flocked to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi’s Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house today, with the extraordinary attendance reflecting the spirit of ‘Bangsa Johor’ and the close relationship between the state government and the people.
The crowd started gathering as early as 8 am to enjoy the festive atmosphere, with 37 stalls offering a variety of traditional and modern dishes.
Among the dishes are ketupat, lemang, rendang, beef biryani, soto and mee rebus, along with a variety of kuih, and hot and cold beverages.
Private executive Nor Intan Ilyana Rahman, 34, said that organising an open house offers a more meaningful experience as it provides a space for direct interaction between leaders and the people.
“It’s not often that we can share space in such a relaxed atmosphere. The large turnout today shows that the people are comfortable and appreciate a more open approach,” she said when met at Saujana, the official residence of the Johor MB here.
Clerk G. Tashweena, 29, described the atmosphere as a true reflection of the harmony of the community in this state.
“All races sitting at the same table, sharing food, and greeting each other without any barriers. This reflects a unity that occurs naturally, not artificially,” she said.
Meanwhile, retiree Lim Siew Eng, 62, said that the approach of reaching out to the people through such programmes has a direct impact on building confidence in the state leadership.
“When leaders meet with the people in such an atmosphere, it fosters a sense of mutual trust. Programmes like this make the people truly feel appreciated,” she said.
The open house is not just a platform to enjoy dishes during Aidilfitri, but rather serves as a social space that brings together people from various backgrounds, thereby strengthening community networks.
Earlier, Onn Hafiz also took the time to mingle and take photographs with guests, adding to the warm atmosphere of the event.
